title: ferray-io: save fails on non-contiguous arrays — should auto-contiguify
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

save() returns an error if the array is not contiguous: 'cannot save non-contiguous array to .npy (make contiguous first)'. NumPy transparently copies non-contiguous arrays to a contiguous buffer before writing. Users who transpose an array and then try to save it get an unhelpful error. Either: (1) auto-contiguify via as_standard_layout() before writing, or (2) iterate elements in logical order and write them (which is what savetxt already does via as_slice check).
